MEDB110OphthalmologicExamNASA Medical Requirements Integration Document for ophthalmologic examination. Captures comprehensive eye examination data for monitoring Spaceflight Associated Neuro-ocular Syndrome (SANS).Questionnairehttps://mitre.org/fhir/space-health/Questionnaire/medb-1-10-ophthalmologic-exam#0.5.12

Initial Value: Coding = NASA MRID Medical Disciplines#ophthalmology "Ophthalmology"

extension.valueString: The astronaut/crewmember subject of this medical requirement

name: MEDB110OphthalmologicExam
title: MEDB 1.10 Ophthalmologic Examination
description: NASA Medical Requirements Integration Document for ophthalmologic examination. Captures comprehensive eye examination data for monitoring Spaceflight Associated Neuro-ocular Syndrome (SANS).
purpose: To assess pre- and postflight ocular status and monitor for Spaceflight Associated Neuro-ocular Syndrome (SANS)
copyright: NASA Johnson Space Center - Medical Requirements Integration Documents (MRIDs) JSC28913

initial.valueString: To assess pre- and postflight ocular status, establish baseline measurements, and monitor for Spaceflight Associated Neuro-ocular Syndrome (SANS) including optic disc edema, globe flattening, choroidal folds, and refractive changes.
Visual acuity, refraction, IOP, fundoscopy, OCT, visual fields

Comprehensive ophthalmologic examination including visual acuity, refraction, intraocular pressure, slit lamp examination, dilated fundoscopy, optical coherence tomography (OCT), and visual field testing.
AME L-9/6 m, L-90 to L-30 days

code.display: Visual acuity best corrected Right eye
Visual acuity best corrected Left eye

initial.valueString: Fundoscopy using Fundoscope onboard ISS. Images reviewed by ground team for SANS monitoring.
R+1 to R+3 days, R+30 days, R+180 days

item.text: N/A - Included as part of Medical Operations overall debrief
